audit_items.proof_trace: audioreach_alloc_graph_pkt() succeeds at line 66, storing a kzalloc'd gpr_pkt into graph->graph. When idr_alloc fails at line 75, the error path at lines 77-80 calls kfree(graph) to free the outer struct but never calls kfree(graph->graph), leaking the separately allocated packet buffer. kref_init (line 84) is only reached after idr_alloc succeeds, so the normal release path q6apm_put_audioreach_graph (which does call kfree(graph->graph) at line 141) is never invoked on this error path.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A TOCTOU race allows two concurrent callers to both pass the idr_find check at line 45 (lock released between find and alloc), both allocate graph->graph, and then the second idr_alloc fails with -ENOSPC, leaking the gpr_pkt buffer; repeatedly triggering concurrent graph opens with the same graph_id causes unbounded kernel heap memory loss.
